item.title: Oral Peptides
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Greenwood Senior Center\, 525 N. 85th St.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Peptides are amino acids that work on proteins in your body\, and are one of the hottest topics in healthcare right now. This talk with Dr. Connie Amundsen provides answers to your questions about oral peptides: how do they differ from injected peptides such as GLP-1 receptor agonists (Wegovy\, Ozempic)\, how do they work\, are they safe\, what conditions are commonly treated\, are they affordable\, and how long does it take to see results? RSVP: 206.297.0875. \nWednesday\, March 25\, 1-2pm. GSC. Free.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

item.title: The Original Food and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Join Savor Seattle for Seattle’s original food and cultural tour of Pike Place Market! On their signature tour, they will take you on a journey through the lively and bustling days in the heart of Seattle: Pike Place Market. While their guides give you the most delicious tastings at eight different partners. You’ll also learn the history of the Market and hear the stories of the small-batch food and craft producers that make this place one of Seattle’s most beloved landmarks. Find out why fish fly, why a pig is our mascot, how the “Original” Starbucks was founded, how an alleyway became covered in gum, what made Pike Place Market one of Anthony Bourdain’s favorite places, and many more pieces of info Seattle Savor guides we’ll be thrilled to share with you while you savor culinary offerings. Once you’re done, you’ll be equipped and ready to dive deeper into the historic neighborhood. Tours are available daily, starting at 10am and 2

From Pike Place Market Events

item.title: Cate Osborn
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Comedian\, historian\, and bestselling author Cate Osborn brings a special program derived from her live touring show\, Wildly Unprepared. This evening features a curated portion of the performance\, exploring the strange and surprising history of magic\, spectacle\, and belief\, along with storytelling and conversation about curiosity\, culture\, and the stories we inherit. \nWhile this program draws from the larger theatrical show\, it is designed as a standalone event created especially for Town Hall audiences. The show will be followed by a Q&A with Cate.

item.title: Duo Comedy Improv Showcase
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley

Wednesdays from 8pm - 9:30pm Seattle’s only open mic for improv. Two-person improv teams! Double your pleasure, double the fun. Seattle’s one and only open mic dedicated to two-person improv teams! Every week at Unexpected Productions, two-person teams take the stage to create hilarious, fast-paced, totally unscripted comedy right before your eyes. No scripts, no safety nets—just pure improv magic. At Duos, everyone shines. Newer performers sharpen their game. Seasoned improvisers experiment, take risks, and get wonderfully weird. The results? Quick-witted scenes, surprising twists, and bold choices that feel impossible until you watch them happen live. Whether you dream of being the next Key & Peele, Tina Fey & Amy Poehler, or a chaotic tag-team like Deadpool & Wolverine, this is your chance to take the mic and run with it. All experience levels are welcome. Bring a friend, form a team, and give Seattle a show they won’t forget. To sign up your duo, email duoshowcase@gmail.com . Looki

From Pike Place Market Events

item.title: The Original Food and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Join Savor Seattle for Seattle’s original food and cultural tour of Pike Place Market! On their signature tour, they will take you on a journey through the lively and bustling days in the heart of Seattle: Pike Place Market. While their guides give you the most delicious tastings at eight different partners. You’ll also learn the history of the Market and hear the stories of the small-batch food and craft producers that make this place one of Seattle’s most beloved landmarks. Find out why fish fly, why a pig is our mascot, how the “Original” Starbucks was founded, how an alleyway became covered in gum, what made Pike Place Market one of Anthony Bourdain’s favorite places, and many more pieces of info Seattle Savor guides we’ll be thrilled to share with you while you savor culinary offerings. Once you’re done, you’ll be equipped and ready to dive deeper into the historic neighborhood. Tours are available daily, starting at 10am and 2

item.title: Comprehensive Plan Office Hours- Magnolia Public Library
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Magnolia Public Library\, 2801 34th Ave W\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

From the Office of Councilman Kettle: \nRight now\, the Office of Planning and Community Development is putting together four specific subarea plans: the Downtown Regional Plan\, the Northgate plan\, and two Manufacturing-Industrial Center plans. All of these plans are intended to come to Council to be voted on in late summer-early fall\, and you can sign up here for OPCD updates. \nIn the meantime\, my office will be hosting several Comprehensive Plan-specific office hours in March and April. \nThese office hours will follow our standard rules: sign in\, each speaker / group gets 10-15 minutes (depending on how many people have signed up)\, and will be first come\, first serve. \nYou can use my Instagram or Facebook to track updates.

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Boutique Bellydancersize Classes
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Blue (upper) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Explore the ancient & mysterious art of belly dancing\, with Boutique Bellydancersize classes! Specialized classes. Superior core workout. Strengthens & coordinates muscle groups in the trunk\, spine & arms. Improves posture & flexibility. Students will learn the basic steps\, undulations\, shimmies\, isolations\, tuxseem and more. All Ages. Group classes at Phinney Center. A focused gym that provides a sense of connection. Zaphara’s classes go beyond physical fitness\, including a broader sense of self care\, including sleep\, nutrition\, skin health\, stress management & socializing. Starts the 1st Thursday of the Month 7:00 pm – 8:00 pm – Beginners 7:45 pm – 9:00 pm Intermediate – Advanced 4 weeks: $80\, Drop-ins: $27 per class Taught by Zaphara\, Master Bellydance Instructor & Performer Sign up and pay online: bellydancingbyzaphara.com/classes

item.title: It Goes On
item.description:

item.title: Loose Cannons Improv
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley

Thursdays from 8pm - 9:30pm Loose Cannons is Seattle improv at its most unpredictable. Every performance at Unexpected Productions in Pike Place Market is a one-of-a-kind experience: exciting, unexpected, a little dangerous, and always ridiculously fun. This is the show where anything can happen and usually does. Part One: Part One: The night kicks off with a unique performance, setting the performers in a new format with special guest improvisers showcasing their signature style or a never-before-seen format. Your suggestions fuel the fire, and the improvisers will ignite the stage, bringing your ideas to life. Part Two: The second half launches from a single audience suggestion, setting the performers in motion. It might start with a song, a monologue, or a rapid-fire group connection game. Whatever emerges will inspire spontaneous stories, surprising twists, and big comedic swings. Loose Cannons is fueled by audience ideas, fearless performers, and the thrill of not knowing what hap

From Pike Place Market Events

item.title: Veteran Benefits 101 Workshop Series
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Labateyah Youth Home\, 9010 13th Ave NW\, Seattle\, WA\, 98117\, United States

Our Native Veterans & Elders Programs are teaming up with the WA’s VA to bring you a Veteran Benefits 101 Workshop series! Whether you are looking to learn about your eligibility or ready to start an application onsite with an accredited Veteran Service Officer\, we are here to support you! Share this workshop series with the veterans in your community! Event Details When: The last Friday of each month Time: 11 am to 1 pm Where: Labateyah Youth Home\, 9010 13th Ave NW\, Seattle\, WA 98117 Upcoming Workshop Topics March 27: Veterans Benefits Administration 101 (Disability Compensation\, Education\, Home Loans\, Burials\, & more) April 24: Veterans Health Administration 101 (VA Hospital & Medical Care) May 29: Veteran Service Officer Networking & Additional Benefits 101 What to Expect Lunch provided for attendees Gift cards available for veterans\, veteran spouses\, or caretakers of veterans (Limit 1 per household) Transportation is available\, if needed Registration is encouraged to help us prepare: https://forms.office.com/r/KrRTQ86ZPr   Questions? Please email us at veterans@unitedindians.org Note: Workshop topics and schedules are subject to change. #SeattleVeterans #SeattleEvents #WashingtonVA #SeattleSeniors #VeteranBenefits #VABenefits #ServiceOfficers #VeteranSupport #MilitaryFamily #Veterans101 #VeteranResources

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Insider’s Breakfast and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Get ahead of peak crowds and join Savor Seattle as they take you behind the scenes on an early-morning exploration of Pike Place Market. Their guides on this tour are deeply rooted members of the Market community who will truly take you to “Meet The Producer” using their years of experience in the neighborhood. This is an experience you cannot get with any regular tour guide! Feel like a local as you walk and eat your way through the best morning bites! Learn the names and stories of the people who operate the businesses Seattle Savor's is honored to highlight as their guides give you the full history of how Pike Place became The Soul of Seattle. "Watching the Market come to life is a time-honored Seattle tradition we would love for you to witness as we share our years of expertise with you. Come savor mornings at Pike Place Market!" Tours are available on Monday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday and Sunday from 8am - 10:30pm Book a Tour

From Pike Place Market Events

item.title: The Original Food and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Join Savor Seattle for Seattle’s original food and cultural tour of Pike Place Market! On their signature tour, they will take you on a journey through the lively and bustling days in the heart of Seattle: Pike Place Market. While their guides give you the most delicious tastings at eight different partners. You’ll also learn the history of the Market and hear the stories of the small-batch food and craft producers that make this place one of Seattle’s most beloved landmarks. Find out why fish fly, why a pig is our mascot, how the “Original” Starbucks was founded, how an alleyway became covered in gum, what made Pike Place Market one of Anthony Bourdain’s favorite places, and many more pieces of info Seattle Savor guides we’ll be thrilled to share with you while you savor culinary offerings. Once you’re done, you’ll be equipped and ready to dive deeper into the historic neighborhood. Tours are available daily, starting at 10am and 2

item.title: A Town Hall on Security and Surveillance
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Join Mayor Wilson and Executive Director of the South Seattle Emerald\, Florangela Davila for a conversation on security\, surveillance\, and their impacts on Seattle’s communities. This event brings Mayor Wilson on stage and in dialogue for a shared look at the challenges and opportunities shaping the city right now. The evening will begin with a moderated conversation\, followed by time for audience Q&A. Together\, we’ll explore questions of safety\, privacy\, and public trust — and consider how policy decisions resonate across the city’s creative and civic landscape. Above all\, this is a chance to gather: to listen\, exchange ideas\, and reflect together on how Seattle can move forward in this moment.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: An Evening with Stephanie Anne Johnson
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: The Rabbit Box, 94 Pike Street Unit 11

The Rabbit Box is excited to present An Evening with Stephanie Anne Johnson , accompanied by Jeff Fielder . They will perform two sets: One set will be the new album ‘Sing, baby!’ performed in its entirety. The second set will all be cover songs; some you’ve never heard Stephanie perform before! Pacific Northwest Americana songwriter and performer Stephanie Anne Johnson ’ s (they/them) voice is like a prayer. It’s the kind of sound that makes you feel renewed, refreshed, and in love with the higher spirits of the planet. It’s a sound you can bask in, that can wash over you and regenerate the best of you all at once. And they have wowed judges on the immensely popular NBC TV series, The Voice, showcasing their gifts. item.title: Unexpected Productions: Seattle Theatresports Improv
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 9:30 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley

Fridays & Saturdays from 9:30pm - 11pm Seattle’s high-energy improv battle where your suggestions shape every scene. Fast, funny, unpredictable comedy Fri. & Sat. 9:30 Welcome to Seattle’s funnest improv comedy show, a high-energy, no-rules showdown where your ideas shape every scene. Seattle Theatresports at Unexpected Productions’ Market Theater in Pike Place Market is part comedy battle, part game night, and completely powered by the crowd. Boo the judges. Cheer the Gum Wall race and laugh til it hurts! Every show, two teams of top Seattle improvisers go head to head, performing games, stories, characters, and scenes inspired entirely by your suggestions. Judges score the rounds, decide the winners, and shape the fun (as we boo them). No scripts. No safety nets. Just fast thinking, bold choices, and pure creative mayhem. This isn’t your parents’ improv show, unless your parents were super cool. Buy Tickets

item.title: DUNE Mini-Comics Night
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 1 PM

Join us today for the 100th edition of DUNE with an extra long DUNE day! On Saturday\, March 28th participants will be creating comics and illustrations for the 100th issue of the mini-comic anthology DUNE. Hugo House on Capitol Hill who partnered with the organizer Marc Palm in 2025 will host an eight hour long event celebrating this milestone. Hugo House is large enough to accommodate 50-80+ participants normally\, more are expected for issue 100. Be prepared for some elbow to elbow space. All are welcome despite skill level or experience. All artwork must be started and finished within the time allowed in black & white and totally analog. A special edition book will be published with all the submitted work and available to only contributors at the following month's meeting on Tuesday April 28th. Dune – Mini-Comics Night 100 will take place on Saturday\, March 28th from 1pm – 9pm. Two drink minimum is required from the soda/beer/wine bar located in Hugo House. Bring your own materials and ideas. Each participant will be asked to donate $10 – $20 (cash or digital payment) towards the cost of printing a special edition book. There will motivational tunes from a live DJ spinning records\, a limited amount of snacks available\, as well as an "Inspiration Packet" given out to the first 100 contributors.  Contact the organizer/publisher Marc Palm – selfsatisfiedbooks@gmail.com or Hugo House welcome@hugohouse.org for more information. DUNE 100 Day 1-9 PM Hugo House 1634 11th Ave\, Seattle\, WA 98122 Preregistering for this event is highly recommend! Grab your free ticket below! Free admission to this event is made possible with support from 4Culture.

From Hugo House

item.title: Insider’s Breakfast and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Get ahead of peak crowds and join Savor Seattle as they take you behind the scenes on an early-morning exploration of Pike Place Market. Their guides on this tour are deeply rooted members of the Market community who will truly take you to “Meet The Producer” using their years of experience in the neighborhood. This is an experience you cannot get with any regular tour guide! Feel like a local as you walk and eat your way through the best morning bites! Learn the names and stories of the people who operate the businesses Seattle Savor's is honored to highlight as their guides give you the full history of how Pike Place became The Soul of Seattle. "Watching the Market come to life is a time-honored Seattle tradition we would love for you to witness as we share our years of expertise with you. Come savor mornings at Pike Place Market!" Tours are available on Monday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday and Sunday from 8am - 10:30pm Book a Tour

item.title: The Original Food and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Join Savor Seattle for Seattle’s original food and cultural tour of Pike Place Market! On their signature tour, they will take you on a journey through the lively and bustling days in the heart of Seattle: Pike Place Market. While their guides give you the most delicious tastings at eight different partners. You’ll also learn the history of the Market and hear the stories of the small-batch food and craft producers that make this place one of Seattle’s most beloved landmarks. Find out why fish fly, why a pig is our mascot, how the “Original” Starbucks was founded, how an alleyway became covered in gum, what made Pike Place Market one of Anthony Bourdain’s favorite places, and many more pieces of info Seattle Savor guides we’ll be thrilled to share with you while you savor culinary offerings. Once you’re done, you’ll be equipped and ready to dive deeper into the historic neighborhood. Tours are available daily, starting at 10am and 2

item.title: Songwriter Showcase w/Kristin Chambers\, Karen Laura Peters\, Allison Preisinger\, Fae Wiedenhoeft
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Brick (lower) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, 98103\, United States

SFS presents an evening of music from 4 accomplished local songwriters! Kristin Chambers’ warm\, soulful style blends jazz\, pop\, and folk influences into a captivating sound. With a background in classical voice and a natural talent for crafting compelling narratives\, her music has found its way to stages and screens\, with placements on shows like “Better Call Saul\,” “The Young and the Restless\,” and “Love is Blind.” Her latest release\, “Kristin Chambers Select Songs Vol. 1\,” is a curated journey through her musical evolution as a songwriter thus far. Karen Laura Peters is a singing cellist with a collaborative nature and a gift for sharing her heart. While training at Oberlin Conservatory\, she fell in love with folk music and began writing her own songs\, joining a band (Her Treehouse) that was featured in NPR’s All Songs Considered blog in 2019. Peters is presently a member of the indie-soul band Queen of Hearts and co-founder of Momentum\, an organization that helps musicians feel inspired\, connected\, and empowered on their music journey. Allison Preisinger is a Berklee College of Music graduate known for evocative\, Americana-infused sounds and unique collaborations like her recent work on a project with the National Park Service. 2025 saw the release of her fourth album\, “Standing Steady\,” blending original music with poetry from a number of sources. A multi-instrumentalist with a passion for connection\, Preisinger uses art to uplift and share the beauty and complexity of human experience. Fae Wiedenhoeft is an award-winning songwriter and classically trained vocalist who was hired to play Celtic music in Canada at age 23 and has been a professional musician ever since. She tours regularly and has played in 10 countries solo and with her band\, SeaStar. Wiedenhoeft has recorded over two dozen albums and teaches voice\, guitar\, ukulele\, and songwriting. SeaStar’s latest album\, released in 2025\, is called “The In Between.” Doors open at 7 pm for the 7:30 pm concert in the Phinney Center’s Community Hall (lower Brick Building). Tickets are available in advance online\, in-person at prior SFS concerts\, or at the door.

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

item.title: Suzanne Simard with Lynda Mapes
item.description:
Sunday, March 29, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Forest ecologist Suzanne Simard has long been fascinated by the sharing of natural knowledge. From the interconnected root systems she studied in her book Finding the Mother Tree to her ongoing work as an educator\, Simard has learned to see the importance of cooperative efforts to share resources and knowledge. Joined in conversation by Seattle-based nature journalist Lynda Mapes\, Simard expands these connections into a considerate exploration of the elaborate cycles of forest ecosystems\, the challenges they currently face\, and the intergenerational value they can provide through her new book When the Forest Breathes: Renewal and Resilience in the Natural World. \nRaised in a family of loggers committed to sensible forest stewardship\, Simard has watched as modern practices and timber companies have left forests vulnerable to damage and depletion. In her research\, Simard explores the finely honed cycles of regeneration forests inherently use to maintain themselves. From mushrooms breaking down logs to dying elder trees passing their genetic knowledge to younger growth\, When the Forest Breathes presents these cycles as a key component in the protection and preservation of our forests. Working closely with Indigenous communities and the models of responsible forestry they’ve upheld over time\, Simard examines the damage caused by industrialization and wide-scale human intervention– particularly the impact on the overstory’s mother trees that are responsible for sharing intergenerational wisdom and supporting new growth.

From Town Hall Seattle

Event details

item.title: Spring Refresh featuring Screen Printing!
item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, WA\, 98103\, United States

Spring Refresh: A Screen Printing Party and Clothing Drive  Come connect! Join us for Spring Refresh\, a hands-on event where neighbors come together to create\, give\, and care for one another. This evening is both a fun activity and an opportunity to show up for our community!  Screen Printing!  Bring a piece of light colored clothing to participate in screen-printing fun. PNA’s amazing Woodshop built two custom screen-printing presses for the night. (We will also have white t-shirts available for sale.) Choose from two designs:  The iconic PhinneyWood Heart – A bold\, graphic symbol of neighborhood pride and love for local small businesses. The PNA Orca\, designed by PNA Art Club member Chino – A powerful symbol of shared knowledge\, care across generations\, and community traditions. Let’s wear our values and tell the story of a community that shows up for one another. Spring Clothing Drive for the Hot Meal Program  As we gather\, we’ll also be collecting clean\, lightly worn clothing for adults to support the PNA’s Hot Meal Program Community Closet. Men’s clothing is especially needed. Some of our most requested items include: New socks and underwear Lightly used shoes and boots Men’s pants\, especially larger sizes Last year\, the Hot Meal Program served over 21\,000 free meals\, while also connecting neighbors to medical care\, haircuts\, and essential resources. Thanks to you\, this support is possible. When you bring clothing or donate\, you’re directly helping meet real needs in our neighborhood. Tunes\, Snacks & Lemonade  Enjoy music\, light snacks\, and refreshing lemonade while you print\, connect\, and give back. Who’s Invited  You! This all-ages event is open to neighbors\, PNA Members\, local PhinneyWood small business owners\, and anyone curious about the PNA. Come meet your neighbors\, learn what’s happening\, and be part of something meaningful. Event Details  Monday\, March 30 5:00–7:00 pm Phinney Center – Room 7 6532 Phinney Ave N\, Seattle\, WA 98103  Free for members  $10 for non-members Donations welcome to support the PNA programs such as the Hot Meal Program.  Spots are limited—register soon to reserve yours!   RSVP

From Phinney Neighborhood Association (PNA)

item.title: PNA Woodshop\, dedicated to Bill Fenimore
item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Phinney Center Campus: Brick (lower) Building\, 6532 Phinney Ave. N.\, Seattle\, 98103\, United States

Did you know that there is a woodshop in your area? Whether you’re a newcomer to woodworking or an experienced woodworker\, the PNA woodshop is the ideal place to bring your creative projects to life. Whether you’re interested in crafting a bench\, flower bed\, jewelry box\, chair\, or cupboard\, the possibilities are endless. All you need to do is become a PNA member\, attend an orientation\, and complete two safety classes. Once you’ve done that\, you can reserve your shop time and get started on your project.

item.title: Stephen Graham Jones
item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Stephen Graham Jones is the author of bone-chilling novels that include The Only Good Indians\, My Heart Is a Chainsaw\, I Was a Teenage Slasher\, and most recently\, The Buffalo Hunter Hunter\, a historical horror novel tracing the life of a vampire who haunts the fields of the Blackfeet reservation looking for justice. \nA diary\, written in 1912 by a Lutheran pastor\, is discovered within a wall. What it unveils is a slow massacre\, a chain of events that goes back to 217 Blackfeet dead in the snow. Told in transcribed interviews by a Blackfeet named Good Stab\, who shares the narrative of his peculiar life over a series of confessional visits. This is an American Indian revenge story written by one of the new masters of horror\, Stephen Graham Jones.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: On Point Live with Meghna Chakrabarti
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Join KUOW for a special live taping of On Point with Meghna Chakrabarti Tuesday\, March 31 at Town Hall Seattle. \nSeattle’s mayor\, socialist Katie Wilson\, won one of the tightest elections in the city’s history last year. Now\, three months into the job\, how is her progressive agenda taking shape — and what might national Democrats learn from her early leadership? \nJoin On Point host Meghna Chakrabarti on Tuesday\, March 31 at 7:30 p.m. at Town Hall Seattle as she sits down with Mayor Katie Wilson for a special live taping of On Point.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Interbay Summer Camps- The Little Gym
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: The Little Gym- Interbay\, 2213 15th Ave W\, Seattle\, WA\, 98119\, United States

Interbay summer camps are 3 hours of Serious Fun for kiddos 3-10 years of age! Camp consists of 2 hours of gym time\, and 1 hour of snacks and craft time. Every week brings a new and exciting theme for your camper and we always have blocks\, books and coloring pages on standby for those campers that need a break. Camps are held Monday-Wednesday from 1-4pm. We offer special discounted pricing for members and camp pack purchases. Non member pricing is $75 for a single camp pass. More information on pack pricing can be found via our scannable QR code. Priority registration opens February 1st.

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: Kiliii Yüyan
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

When we think of environmental conservation\, we tend to think in terms of emerging technologies and increased data tracking. While technology is one piece of the puzzle\, we are also called to look back and take stock of information that has existed since time immemorial. In Indigenous communities\, knowledge of the environment and how to protect it has been acquired over countless generations. In Guardians of Life: Indigenous Science\, Indigenous Wisdom and Restoring the Planet\, Kiliii Yüyan presents a photographic journey that reveals Indigenous communities to be Earth’s most powerful protectors. \nYüyan’s photography is informed by the collective wisdom of more than two dozen Indigenous voices\, including activist Quannah Chasinghorse and Palauan statesman Tommy Remengesau Jr. Yüyan provides striking imagery to assist in telling the stories of how traditional knowledge and modern innovation come together as a planetary safeguard. Guardians of Life takes us all over the world\, from the coral reefs in Palau\, to Mongolia’s steppes\, to the Amazon rainforest\, even to beds of sea ice in Greenland.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: First Thursday - April
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: MOHAI\, MOHAI - 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Visit MOHAI on the First Thursday of every month for free admission to the museum’s permanent exhibits between 5—8 pm. \nFree admission includes access to all of MOHAI’s award winning exhibits\, including our temporary displays Riding Together: 135 Years of Cycling in Seattle\, TADAIMA: I’m Home and special exhibit Kickstands Up! 125 Years of Motorcycling in the PNW. Advance online tickets are not required\, but may be reserved below. \nFree admission also includes access to two special presentations of Living Voices in the Joshua Green Theater at 5:30 and 6:30pm. Seats are first-come first-served.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

item.title: Living Voices: Winning Her Wings
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Joshua Green Foundation Theater\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

They flew planes. They fought prejudice. They made history. Winning Her Wings brings to life the groundbreaking Women Airforce Service Pilots (WASPs). Follow journeys of women who faced and broke barriers together\, fighting for justice in a time of global conflict and domestic prejudice. Discover how they soared—despite the odds. \nThis new multimedia performance by Living Voices blends live theatre and archival film to honor the women who served with courage. Premiering in 2026 for Women’s History Month. This event takes place during our Free First Thursday hours. Seats are first come first served. \nLiving Voices combines live theatrical performances with archival film\, turning history into a moving personal journey. This performance will take place in the Joshua Green Foundation Theater on MOHAI’s second floor.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Psychedelic Salon: Psychedelics & Disordered Eating
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

A conversation about the emerging potential of psychedelics\, particularly psilocybin\, in addressing eating disorders—a psychiatric area marked by persistent treatment challenges. This Salon dives into groundbreaking research showing how psychedelics disrupt entrenched thought patterns\, ease cognitive rigidity\, and foster self-compassion and emotional reconnection. April Pride and invited experts will discuss the limitations of current treatments and how psychedelic therapies could offer new hope. Attendees will leave with a deeper understanding of how psychedelic-assisted therapy might revolutionize the path to healing for those struggling with disordered eating. \nDr. Amanda Downey is a pediatrician and psychiatrist who cares for adolescents and young adults with eating disorders. She works with patients and their families to develop a treatment plan that honors their unique strengths and challenges. Through education\, advocacy\, and research\, she strives to dismantle the systems that increase risk for eating disorders and improve evidence-based eating disorder treatments. She is the assistant medical director of the UCSF Eating Disorders Program and a member of the UCSF Translational Psychedelic Research Program (TrPR).

From Town Hall Seattle

Event details

item.title: J. Kenji López-Alt and Tessa Lark present Tasting Notes
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

If nothing is truly new\, what is creativity? \nIn this special live event\, New York Times bestselling author and James Beard Award–winning chef J. Kenji López-Alt joins Grammy-nominated violinist and composer Tessa Lark for an evening of cooking\, music\, and conversation. Moderated by renowned food writer\, Serious Eats founder\, and Special Sauce host Ed Levine\, the program explores how creatives build on tradition\, transform influence\, and turn identity into expression. \nOn stage\, Kenji brings his signature curiosity to the kitchen\, while Lark\, a Kentucky-born musician who bridges bluegrass roots with classical virtuosity\, reveals how seemingly distant traditions can combine to form brand new sounds. Through live culinary demonstration and musical performance\, this special edition of Kenji’s Tasting Notes reflects on how cultures and ideas blend\, and how originality is less invention and more a reinterpretation of our shared cultural histories. \n\n WHAT TO EXPECT Get ready for a multisensory experience that fuses live music\, cooking demos\, tastings\, and conversation. Think: A stunning Great Hall filled with the sound of Tessa Lark’s violin; curiosity-driven conversation guided by Ed Levine; and interactive\, culinary creativity with J. Kenji Alt-Lopez that is rumored to involve blindfolds\, nose plugs\, and…20 lbs. of gummy bears? You’ll have to join us to find out!

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Lindy West
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

In her new memoir\, Adult Braces\, Lindy West brings readers along on an uproarious cross-country road trip as she unpacks her last few tumultuous years\, rediscovers herself\, and reinvents her marriage. \nThrough Shrill—the book and then the Hulu series—Lindy West became an inspiration. To this day\, she is stopped on the street and hailed as a beacon of empowerment by women who felt badly for not conforming to a narrow set of societal norms—thin\, straight\, compliant. But behind the scenes\, Lindy never felt like she was the self-actualized woman fans made her out to be. When she found herself in the throes of a deep depression\, with her marriage and sense of self-worth hanging in the balance\, she knew she needed to make a change. \nIn Adult Braces\, Lindy shares the story of her rock bottom and of the journey she took to claw her way out of it. With her trademark candor and sense of humor\, she examines her post-Shrill emotional implosion\, her shifting feelings about traditional marriage\, and her search for her long-lost self. She also tracks the highs and lows of her journey\, from eye-opening natural wonders and kitschy roadside attractions to lackluster tourist traps and campground epiphanies. The result is an engaging and laugh-out-loud narrative of becoming as Lindy transforms from a passenger into the active navigator of her own life.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Saturday Family Concert: The Brian Waite Band
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Come sing\, dance\, and laugh with the band that has rocked its way into the hearts of both kids and parents\, as they fly with superheroes and dance on the moon! \nThe Brian Waite Band was born from the idea of creating live rock shows that thrill both parents and children alike. Founded in 2001 by Brian Waite (guitar)\, Todd Gray (drums)\, and Clif Swiggett (bass)\, the trio began touring the West Coast\, and before they knew it\, their music was being played all over the world. Twenty-five years and eight critically-acclaimed albums later\, their interactive shows have evolved into more than just children’s concerts. The Brian Waite Band’s live shows combine musical theater\, imaginative storytelling\, laughs\, and a great rock ‘n roll concert filled with original songs that take the whole family on a “Rock ‘n Roll Adventure.”

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Slavoj Zizek
item.description:
Saturday, April 4, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Join philosopher Slavoj Žižek for a guide to the end times. Is despair the only hope? Can the world escape the clutches of libertarian fascism? Erudite and comic\, ironic and profound\, philosopher Slavoj Žižek has travelled into territory where few of us dare to tread – and at age 76\, he shows no signs of becoming less provocative. As populism continues to grip the globe\, Slavoj joins How To Academy to share his iconoclastic reflections on the state of the world and our prospects for the future. Taking us from Europe and the Middle East into the dark heart of Trump’s America\, he will explore the paradox of ‘liberal fascism’\, peering at the seeping wounds beneath our ‘post-truth age’ to ask: what forms of justice are possible in an age of terrifying disorder? This will be an evening of dark humour to help us survive in a dark era\, with Trump\, Putin\, postmodernism\, and antisemitism all on the cards. Slavoj promises to tell us many things we do not want to hear — and to remind us why he is widely known as the ‘most dangerous philosopher of the West’. Don’t miss it. Slavoj Žižek will sign copies of Liberal Fascisms following the event. Events are offered for informational\, entertainment\, and educational purposes only. Read Town Hall’s Program Content Policy.

From Town Hall Seattle

Invite your family\, friends\, and neighbors to this joyous celebration of hope!

item.title: V.E. Schwab and Cat Clarke with Moira Macdonald
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Layering classic tropes of the mystery genre with a clever modern satire of the publishing industry\, The Ending Writes Itself is the debut novel of “Evelyn Clarke\,” a new writing collaboration between best-selling authors V.E. Schwab and Cat Clarke. Joined in conversation by local author and journalist Moira Macdonald\, Schwab and Clarke explore their co-authoring venture and the twists and turns that await readers in their new take on a locked-room mystery thriller. How will the pages unfold for the six authors isolated with nothing but their own minds\, a diabolical deadline\, and a plot-driving incentive? Starting is often the hardest part. But getting to the end could be murder. \nWorld-renowned best-selling novelist Arthur Fletch is a reclusive genius who captivates readers with iconic protagonists and fiendish twists. So when six struggling authors of different genres receive an invitation to spend a weekend on his private island off the coast of Scotland\, they pack their bags. But the twists have gone further than ever this time\, and the authors are greeted with a shocking secret– Arthur Fletch is dead… and his highly-anticipated last novel remains unfinished. \nDesperate to publish\, Fletch’s agent and editor have summoned these writers hoping one of them can pen a worthy ending. To sweeten the deal\, they pitch them the opportunity of a lifetime. Ghost write the final chapter and win an irresistible prize– a mind-boggling sum of money and a partnership that could relaunch their own careers and guarantee future bestsellers. And with that to motivate them\, surely 72 hours is enough time to finish Fletch’s magnus opus? All they have to do is write…

From Town Hall Seattle

Event details

item.title: Design for Longevity: ADUs and Aging in Place
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Mehdi Reading Room\, 1119 8th Ave\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Join Lili Weigert\, author of Making Space: ADUs for Modern Living\, in conversation with local ADU designers Joe Schneider and Mike Freeman of JAS Design Build. They will explore how thoughtful ADU design can support aging in place\, foster intergenerational living\, and provide resilient housing options for the future. Rebecca Crichton\, Executive Director of Northwest Center for Creative Aging\, will moderate the discussion.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: A Conversation with George Saunders
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

An electric novel from the #1 New York Times bestselling\, Booker Prize-winning author of Lincoln in the Bardo\, taking place at the bedside of an oil company CEO\, in the twilight hours of his life\, as he is ferried from this world into the next. \nNot for the first time\, Jill “Doll” Blaine finds herself hurtling toward earth\, reconstituting as she falls\, right down to her favorite black pumps. She plummets towards her newest charge\, yet another soul she must usher into the afterlife\, and lands headfirst in the circular drive of his ornate mansion. \nShe has performed this sacred duty three hundred and forty-three times since her own death. Her charges\, as a rule\, have been greatly comforted in their final moments. But this charge\, she soon discovers\, isn’t like the others: the powerful K.J. Boone will not be consoled\, because he has nothing to regret. He lived a big\, bold life\, and the world is better for it. Isn’t it? \nVigil transports us\, careening\, through the wild final evening of an epic\, complicated life. Crowds of people and animals — worldly and otherworldly\, alive and dead — arrive\, clamoring for a reckoning. Birds swarm the dying man’s room\, a black calf grazes on the loveseat\, a man from a distant drought-ravaged village materializes\, two oil-business cronies from decades past show up with chilling plans for Boone’s post-death future. \nWith the acuity and explosive imagination we’ve come to expect\, George Saunders takes on the gravest issues of our time — the menace of corporate greed\, the toll of capitalism\, the environmental perils of progress — and\, in the process\, spins a tale that encompasses life and death\, good and evil\, and the thorny question of absolution.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Meet Up Magnolia- virtual networking
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/85388228818?pwd=amdmUEMvcVo2YjhETkxkbGJCa1Q4Zz09

Our member businesses know the value of networking. Join us for our virtual networking event\, Meet Up Magnolia\, on the  2nd Thursday of every month. \nHow does this virtual networking work? You simply log on to the Zoom link and will be paired into one-on-one chats for 8 to 10 minutes each. We will repeat the process for approximately 3 or 4 rounds. You even have the option to follow up after the meeting and send a message to one or more of the people you meet to keep the dialogue going. \nSo many people to meet\, so many professional relationships to build. Join us HERE.

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Terry Tempest Williams (SOLD OUT)
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

In a world so fraught with chaos and uncertainty\, where can we find connection and hope? Author Terry Tempest Williams invites readers to learn about what she calls “The Glorians.” \nIn her latest work\, The Glorians: Visitations from the Holy Ordinary\, Williams explains that Glorians are not distant deities\, but instead ordinary\, often overlooked presences — animals\, plants\, memories\, moments — that reveal our shared vulnerability and interconnectedness with the natural world. The Glorians can be as small as an ant or as commonplace as the night sky. Williams offers both personal and societal insight in her assertion that Glorians have much to teach and remind us about consciousness\, connection\, and courage. \nIn pages that explore subjects like climate change\, finding wonder in the unexpected\, and turning to deep reflection and intention\, The Glorians invites us to imagine a better and more beautiful future in a time of uncertainty.

From Town Hall Seattle

Event details

item.title: Emma Straub
item.description:
Thursday, April 9, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

From New York Times bestselling author of This Time Tomorrow\, an irresistible story about what happens when your teenage fantasy comes true after you’re already an adult. In Emma Straub’s tradition of books “that will make you laugh\, make you cry\, and make you call the people you love” (Emily Henry)\, American Fantasy is a richly textured\, uplifting story about the magic of revisiting youthful feelings\, and the even greater magic of starting anew. \nWhen the American Fantasy cruise ship sets sail for a four-day themed voyage\, aboard are all five members of a famous 1990s boy band\, and three thousand screaming women who have worshipped them for thirty years. \nNewly divorced and with an empty nest\, Annie is on board as a lark to appease her sister. Once a diehard fan of the band as a teen\, her tastes have matured\, and she feels out of place amid the sea of bedazzled\, air-brushed t-shirts bearing the singers’ faces. Yet when the lights come up and the idols of her youth begin to sing before her\, something is unlocked. “Maybe that was nostalgia after all\, the music a direct vein to her childhood\, the least complicated part of her life. A shortcut to happiness.” Between the slushy alcoholic drinks\, the music of her youth\, and the thousands of middle-aged women acting like lovesick teenagers\, Annie finally reconnects to a long-submerged part of herself. By the time she befriends one of the band members — not just a celebrity but someone also in need of a friend — she feels like anything is possible. But a lot can go wrong on a ship ruled by hormones and hope\, frustration and fantasy.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Santa Clarita Valley Youth Orchestra
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

The Santa Clarita Valley Youth Orchestra (SCVYO)\, an innovative Los Angeles–based nonprofit youth orchestra\, makes its Seattle debut in a dynamic evening celebrating collaboration\, creativity\, and cross-disciplinary performance. This special concert brings together three vibrant youth arts communities: SCVYO\, students from the Seattle Youth Symphony\, and dancers from Spectrum Dance Theater Youth Company. The program culminates in a powerful\, staged performance of selections from Georges Bizet’s Carmen Suite No. 1\, featuring live choreography performed alongside the orchestra. The evening opens with string repertoire showcasing the expressive depth and precision of SCVYO and Seattle Youth Symphony musicians\, followed by the electrifying rhythms and dramatic flair of Carmen & more! Audiences will experience music not only as sound\, but as movement and storytelling — an immersive collaboration between young instrumentalists and dancers. Designed for families\, classical music lovers\, and arts supporters alike\, in this performance\, audiences will experience the excitement of seeing young artists from different cities unite on one stage. The result is a celebration of artistic partnership and the transformative power of youth arts education. This concert is free and open to the public. Optional donations are warmly welcomed and will directly support the participating youth arts organizations and the students you see on stage. Events are offered for informational\, entertainment\, and educational purposes only. Read Town Hall’s Program Content Policy.

From Town Hall Seattle

Event details

item.title: for posterity: proof of free will
item.description:
Friday, April 10, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

for posterity: proof of free will is a speculative oral history — a performance staged as a panel discussion reflecting on the end of humanity. Panelists look back at a civilization and environment in collapse\, tracing the chain of events that began when humanity chose to stop reproducing and instead prepare for the last person’s death. \nThis is an exploration of peace in the face of existential crisis. As runaway climate change spiraled uncontrollably and humanity reached the brink of cataclysmic war\, undeniable questions arose: Do we continue? What if we just… stopped? \nThe project asks what happens if we stop resisting mortality — not just individually\, but on a global scale. Under this condition\, the discussion is forced to consider alternative meanings for sex\, existence\, and community\, opening space for new\, subversive questions. If not survival\, then what? If we were to turn off the engines of exploitation\, how would the trauma we carry and the damaged planet transform? How could we practice inter-species care\, care for the planet\, recognition of harm\, and attempt reparation on the largest possible scale? \nIn this world\, what did humanity ultimately achieve through designing its own end?

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Planet Money: A Guide to the Economic Forces That Shape Your Life
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Great Hall\, 1119 Eighth Avenue (enter on Eighth Avenue)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Join the team behind the popular podcast Planet Money and celebrate the publication of their new book\, Planet Money: A Guide to the Economic Forces That Shape Your Life. Book author and longtime contributor Alex Mayyasi\, Planet Money host Kenny Malone\, and visual host Jack Corbett will bring Planet Money’s signature brand of storytelling to the stage and discuss some highlights from the new book\, including unlikely origin stories\, revealing truths about human behavior\, and insightful suggestions for navigating chaotic times drawn from the latest ideas in economics. Daryl Fairweather\, Chief Economist at Redfin\, will be the special guest this evening. Come prepared to engage with the silly and the sublime and ask some questions of your favorite Planet Money hosts and reporters. \nEach book purchased with a ticket comes with an exclusive Planet Money tote bag!

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: History Café: Preserving the Vision of Asahel Curtis
item.description:
Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Faye G. Allen Grand Atrium\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Join Margaret Weatherbee\, Head of Collections at the Washington State Historical Society and History Museum\, for an in-depth look at the digitization and preservation of the Asahel Curtis Collection. This collection features over 42\,000 images by photographer Asahel Curtis (1873-1941) that document the evolution of life in the Pacific Northwest in the early 20th century. \nHistory Café is produced as a partnership between HistoryLink\, The Pacific Northwest Historians Guild\, and MOHAI. \nASL Interpretation and CART captioning are available during the program. In addition\, a limited number of Assistive Listening Devices are available upon request. For more accessibility support\, email programs@mohai.org before the program. \nGrab some food for thought–complimentary snacks and beverages will be provided. \nIf you can’t make it to the museum\, tune in live on the MOHAI YouTube channel to virtually attend this event.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

From City of Seattle — Office of Arts & Cultural Affairs

Event details

item.title: This Art Here
item.description:
Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

What roles do artists play in civil society? What do artists think about when they think about making things in public? Is art a basic need? Join us in conversation with Nakisa Dehpanah\, Titus Ross\, and Imani Sims — three artists pushing towards a future rooted in collaboration\, resilience\, and transformation. \nEach artist is part of the Seattle Office of Arts & Culture’s collective imagining\, “We Still Dream a Future”. This series features more than thirty artists and artist groups performing\, dancing\, installing\, learning\, organizing\, and exhibiting around Downtown\, the C-ID\, Pioneer Square\, Stadium District\, Union\, and King Street Stations this year. \nThe conversation will be hosted by Matthew Offenbacher\, Seattle artist and advisor for ARTS at King Street Station. \nThis program is supported by the City of Seattle Office of Arts & Culture and is part of the commUNITY series\, a selection of free public programs that center on connection\, career development\, and the co-creation of art curated by ARTS at King Street Station Advisors.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Scratch Night with Ollella
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Why don’t we often see symphonic music in clubs\, or rock music in concert halls — and what shifts when we try? \nSymphonic music has evolved through concert halls\, designed to project acoustically in naturally reverberant rooms. Rock music\, by contrast\, was born in clubs: amplified\, built for energy and proximity. These traditions developed in different spaces\, for different audiences\, with different listening experiences. As a cellist working within contemporary songwriting\, Ollella often finds herself standing between these two worlds. So what happens when you bring them together? \nThis talk unpacks what Ollella has learned from merging these genres in her own work\, making what she calls ‘symphonic-indie’ music. She will examine how arrangement\, amplification\, and instrumentation shape emotional impact through performance and recorded music. Rather than choosing one tradition over the other\, this evening explores what new language can emerge when space\, scale\, and sound are considered together.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: As Many Weirdos As Possible
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: The Wyncote NW Forum\, 1119 8th Ave (Entrance off Seneca St.)\, Seattle\, 98101\, United States

Join As Many Weirdos As Possible (AMWAP) for an evening of storytelling and portraiture that brings to life one of the most vibrant chapters of the Pacific Northwest music scene (1985-1995). This live program will feature musicians\, artists\, and community members sharing personal memories\, projected alongside their documentary portraits as part of the ongoing AMWAP project. Drawn from Poser Productions’ mission to preserve and celebrate personal and cultural histories\, this evening invites audiences to engage in a communal reflection on memory\, music\, identity\, and place. Hosted by John Roderick. Best known for his work with The Long Winters\, Harvey Danger\, and The Western State Hurricanes\, John was also a founding member of the Seattle Music Commission and a short-order chef at The Offramp. Storytellers Sheryl Wiser Dejha Colantuono Steve Manning Jesse Higman Susan Silver Hiro Yamamoto With storytelling coaching by Emmett Montgomery Presented by Town Hall Seattle and Poser Productions. Poser Productions is fiscally sponsored by Shunpike. Events are offered for informational\, entertainment\, and educational purposes only. Read Town Hall’s Program Content Policy.

From Town Hall Seattle

item.title: Meet the Cossacks: Seattle's Motorcycle Stunt and Drill Team
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Microsoft Lakefront Pavilion\, MOHAI - 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Dive into the history of the Cossacks\, Seattle’s long strolling Motorcycle Stunt and Drill Team\, with Rob Root and other members. Since 1938\, the Seattle Cossacks have captured the imagination of audiences with great pyramids and other wonders of the motorcycling world. Following the presentation\, visitors are welcome to get up close and personal for photos with the team’s vintage Harleys! \nTickets for this program are free\, but do not include access to MOHAI’s exhibitions. If you would like to see all that MOHAI has to offer during your visit\, you can purchase museum admission here. \nFor more accessibility support\, email programs@mohai.org two weeks before the program.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

item.title: Daybreak Star Community 5K Run and Walk
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

Join the community for a series of monthly 5K runs and walks held at the historic Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center. This free event welcomes participants of all levels\, from competitive runners to those who enjoy a leisurely walk through beautiful scenery. Event Schedule Events take place on the 3rd Sunday of each month. Please note the following upcoming dates: February 15th March 15th April 19th May 17th June 21st July 19th (37th Annual Indian Days Powwow Weekend\, starting at 9:00 AM) Timing and Location Details Start Time: 10:00 AM Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way Seattle\, WA 98199) Meeting Point: Meet at the Powwow Grounds This event is FREE for all! Lace up your shoes and enjoy the scenic trails while connecting with the community! For more information about the venue and organizing group\, please email communications@unitedindians.org

item.title: Paving the Way: Bicycle Infrastructure Tour
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Offsite

Join Merlin Rainwater\, creator of Seattle’s Infamous Redline Guide\, and Tom Fucoloro\, Founder and Editor of the Seattle Bike Blog and author of Biking Uphill in the Rain: The Story of Seattle from behind the Handlebars\, for a bicycle tour exploring the history of Seattle bicycle infrastructure. \nThis tour requires riding a bicycle between multiple sites in Seattle\, totaling close to 10 miles and starting in the Central District. Please bring your own bike. Helmets and lights will be required. For any accessibility support\, or general inquiries\, email programs@mohai.org.

Event details

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Veteran Benefits 101 Workshop Series
item.description:
Friday, April 24, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Labateyah Youth Home\, 9010 13th Ave NW\, Seattle\, WA\, 98117\, United States

Our Native Veterans & Elders Programs are teaming up with the WA’s VA to bring you a Veteran Benefits 101 Workshop series! Whether you are looking to learn about your eligibility or ready to start an application onsite with an accredited Veteran Service Officer\, we are here to support you! Share this workshop series with the veterans in your community! Event Details When: The last Friday of each month Time: 11 am to 1 pm Where: Labateyah Youth Home\, 9010 13th Ave NW\, Seattle\, WA 98117 Upcoming Workshop Topics March 27: Veterans Benefits Administration 101 (Disability Compensation\, Education\, Home Loans\, Burials\, & more) April 24: Veterans Health Administration 101 (VA Hospital & Medical Care) May 29: Veteran Service Officer Networking & Additional Benefits 101 What to Expect Lunch provided for attendees Gift cards available for veterans\, veteran spouses\, or caretakers of veterans (Limit 1 per household) Transportation is available\, if needed Registration is encouraged to help us prepare: https://forms.office.com/r/KrRTQ86ZPr   Questions? Please email us at veterans@unitedindians.org Note: Workshop topics and schedules are subject to change. #SeattleVeterans #SeattleEvents #WashingtonVA #SeattleSeniors #VeteranBenefits #VABenefits #ServiceOfficers #VeteranSupport #MilitaryFamily #Veterans101 #VeteranResources

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Kickstands Up! All Motorcycle Club Day
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: MOHAI\, MOHAI - 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Inviting all motorcycle clubs to roll on down to MOHAI! Indicate your Motorcycle Cub membership and receive $5 off admission all day\, and explore Kickstands Up: 125 Years of Motorcycling in the Pacific Northwest with gallery talks by exhibit co-curators from the Pacific Northwest Museum of Motorcycling from 2-4pm. \nSpots are limited and are assigned on a first-come\, first-served basis. \nFor accessibility support\, please contact programs@mohai.org two weeks before the event.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: Just Shred It!
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Key Bank\, 2401 34th Ave W\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

April 25\, 2026; Just Shred IT! 10 AM to 1 PM\, KeyBank parking lot\, W. McGraw St and 34th Ave W. \nEnter off W. McGraw St.     All Residences and Businesses are invited to bring their important papers \nto be commercially shredded. Save time and effort while gaining peace of mind that your important papers and information are disposed of in a safe and professional manner.  Old tax records\, old files\, save time and effort.  A $10 donation per box/bag is suggested.  Donations benefit our 501 (c) (3) Rotary Club of Magnolia Charitable Foundation.  We will provide you a tax receipt. First come first served. \nQuestions\, please contact Bryce McWalter\, 206-283-2037 or info@magnoliarotary.org.

From Discover Magnolia

item.description:

Help us grow this March during Forest Garden Land & Community Tending Days at the Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center! 🌱✨ \nCome support Indigenous food sovereignty by tending to traditional plants like camas and huckleberry. item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 30, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Interbay Summer Camps- The Little Gym
item.description:
Friday, May 1, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: The Little Gym- Interbay\, 2213 15th Ave W\, Seattle\, WA\, 98119\, United States

Interbay summer camps are 3 hours of Serious Fun for kiddos 3-10 years of age! Camp consists of 2 hours of gym time\, and 1 hour of snacks and craft time. Every week brings a new and exciting theme for your camper and we always have blocks\, books and coloring pages on standby for those campers that need a break. Camps are held Monday-Wednesday from 1-4pm. We offer special discounted pricing for members and camp pack purchases. Non member pricing is $75 for a single camp pass. More information on pack pricing can be found via our scannable QR code. Priority registration opens February 1st.

item.title: Pet First Aid
item.description:
Saturday, May 2, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Metro Dog\, 4020 23rd Ave W\, Seattle\, WA\, 98119\, United States

Join us for a day of the most comprehensive pet first aid course available! Our Walks ‘n’ Wags certified instructors offer a day of fun and learning. Classes are intended for pet care professionals\, dog and cat owners\, rescue workers\, first responders\, groomers and shelter workers. Course completion offers continuing education credits for PSI\, IAABC\, IACP and CBCC-KA members. This class has received the Animal Wellness Magazine Stamp of Approval and a Modern Dog Fave Find. We work with live animals in class.

item.title: Forest Garden Land & Community Tending Days
item.description:
Thursday, May 7, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

Help us grow this March during Forest Garden Land & Community Tending Days at the Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center! 🌱✨ \nCome support Indigenous food sovereignty by tending to traditional plants like camas and huckleberry. The plants grown here nourishes our elders\, youth\, families\, and ceremonies. Help us build a future rooted in culture\, care\, and connection! 🍓 \nQuestions? Email Shameka.Gagnier@gmail.com or call/text (720) 212 7113 📞

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, May 7, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Forest Garden Land & Community Tending Days
item.description:
Friday, May 8, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

Help us grow this March during Forest Garden Land & Community Tending Days at the Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center! 🌱✨ \nCome support Indigenous food sovereignty by tending to traditional plants like camas and huckleberry. The plants grown here nourishes our elders\, youth\, families\, and ceremonies. Help us build a future rooted in culture\, care\, and connection! 🍓 \nQuestions? Email Shameka.Gagnier@gmail.com or call/text (720) 212 7113 📞

item.title: Sensory Friendly Morning
item.description:
Saturday, May 9, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: MOHAI\, MOHAI - 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

This event welcomes adults and families who may benefit from a low-sensory environment to explore the museum. During this event\, the museum will be closed to the general public and the lights and sounds in the gallery will be lowered. A sensory map and information about what to expect during your visit to MOHAI will be provided via email following registration. Spots are limited so register early! \nHoras más tranquilas\nFecha/hora: sábado 9 de mayo\, de 9:00 a. m. a 11:00 a. m.\nCosto: gratuito con inscripción previa \nEste evento da la bienvenida a adultos y familias que puedan beneficiarse de un entorno con bajo nivel sensorial para explorar el museo. Durante el evento\, el museo permanecerá cerrado al público en general\, y se bajarán las luces y los sonidos de la galería. Tras la inscripción\, se enviará por correo electrónico un mapa sensorial e información sobre lo que puede esperar durante su visita al Museum of History & Industry (MOHAI\, Museo de Historia e Industria). Los cupos son limitados\, así que inscríbase pronto.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Thursday, May 14, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/85388228818?pwd=amdmUEMvcVo2YjhETkxkbGJCa1Q4Zz09

Our member businesses know the value of networking. Join us for our virtual networking event\, Meet Up Magnolia\, on the  2nd Thursday of every month. \nHow does this virtual networking work? You simply log on to the Zoom link and will be paired into one-on-one chats for 8 to 10 minutes each. We will repeat the process for approximately 3 or 4 rounds. You even have the option to follow up after the meeting and send a message to one or more of the people you meet to keep the dialogue going. \nSo many people to meet\, so many professional relationships to build. Join us HERE.

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, May 14, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star
